name: Git Operation Tests
theme: Tests for all git.sh functions using isolated temporary git repositories

criteria.description: meta-tests/test-git.bats exists, is a valid bats file, and loads the test helper via 'load helpers/test-helper'
testSteps: Verify meta-tests/test-git.bats exists and is non-empty
Verify it starts with #!/usr/bin/env bats shebang
Verify it contains "load 'helpers/test-helper'" or equivalent

criteria.description: All tests in test-git.bats pass when run via 'bats meta-tests/test-git.bats'
testSteps: Run: bats meta-tests/test-git.bats
Verify exit code is 0
Verify no tests show as 'not ok'

criteria.description: The full meta-tests suite still passes (no regressions): 'bash meta-tests/run.sh' exits 0
testSteps: Run: bash meta-tests/run.sh
Verify exit code is 0

criteria.description: test-git.bats contains at least 20 @test blocks covering all 6 git.sh functions
testSteps: Count lines matching '@test ' in test-git.bats
Verify count >= 20

criteria.description: Test: git_create_harness_branch creates a 'harness/{slug}' branch from main
testSteps: Verify a @test exists that calls git_create_harness_branch with a slug argument
Verify it asserts the branch 'harness/{slug}' exists after the call
Run the test and verify it passes

criteria.description: Test: git_create_harness_branch returns branch name on stdout
testSteps: Verify a @test exists that captures the output of git_create_harness_branch
Verify the test asserts the returned string matches 'harness/{slug}'

criteria.description: Test: git_create_harness_branch is idempotent -- re-running checks out existing branch without error
testSteps: Verify a @test exists that calls git_create_harness_branch twice with the same slug
Verify the test asserts the second call succeeds (exit 0) and the repo is on the correct branch

criteria.description: Test: git_create_sprint_branch creates '{harness-branch}-sprint-NN' with zero-padded sprint number
testSteps: Verify a @test exists that calls git_create_sprint_branch with a harness branch and sprint number
Verify it asserts the branch name includes the zero-padded sprint number (e.g., sprint-03)

criteria.description: Test: git_create_sprint_branch returns branch name on stdout and leaves repo on sprint branch
testSteps: Verify a @test checks the return value of git_create_sprint_branch
Verify it also asserts the current branch is the sprint branch after the call

criteria.description: Test: git_create_sprint_branch deletes stale sprint branch from prior failed attempt before recreating
testSteps: Verify a @test exists that pre-creates a sprint branch, then calls git_create_sprint_branch
Verify the test asserts the old branch is replaced (not duplicated) and the new branch is on the harness branch's HEAD

criteria.description: Test: git_merge_sprint performs a --no-ff merge of the sprint branch into the harness branch
testSteps: Verify a @test sets up a harness branch and sprint branch with at least one commit, then calls git_merge_sprint
Verify it asserts the current branch is the harness branch after merge
Verify the merge commit exists (git log shows the merge message)

criteria.description: Test: git_merge_sprint creates annotated tag 'harness/sprint-NN/pass'
testSteps: Verify a @test calls git_merge_sprint and then checks that the tag 'harness/sprint-NN/pass' exists
Verify the tag points to the merge commit

criteria.description: Test: git_merge_sprint deletes the sprint branch after merging
testSteps: Verify a @test calls git_merge_sprint and then verifies the sprint branch no longer exists
Verify with: git rev-parse --verify {sprint-branch} should fail

criteria.description: Test: git_merge_sprint returns the merge SHA on stdout
testSteps: Verify a @test captures the output of git_merge_sprint
Verify the returned SHA matches HEAD on the harness branch after merge

criteria.description: Test: git_merge_sprint commits any uncommitted changes on the sprint branch before merging
testSteps: Verify a @test creates uncommitted changes (e.g., writes to a file) on the sprint branch before calling git_merge_sprint
Verify the uncommitted changes are present in the harness branch after merge

criteria.description: Test: git_fail_sprint_attempt creates a tag 'harness/sprint-NN/attempt-N'
testSteps: Verify a @test sets up a sprint branch, calls git_fail_sprint_attempt, and then checks the tag exists

criteria.description: Test: git_fail_sprint_attempt returns to the harness branch and deletes the sprint branch
testSteps: Verify a @test calls git_fail_sprint_attempt and then asserts the current branch is the harness branch
Verify the sprint branch no longer exists after the call

criteria.description: Test: git_fail_sprint_attempt handles uncommitted changes via stash (does not fail with dirty working tree)
testSteps: Verify a @test creates uncommitted changes on the sprint branch before calling git_fail_sprint_attempt
Verify the function completes without error (exit 0)
Verify the working directory is clean after returning to harness branch

criteria.description: Test: git_commit_harness_state commits harness-state directory changes with the given message
testSteps: Verify a @test modifies a file in harness-state/, calls git_commit_harness_state with a message
Verify git log shows a new commit with the expected message

criteria.description: Test: git_commit_harness_state is a no-op when nothing has changed in harness-state/
testSteps: Verify a @test calls git_commit_harness_state with no pending changes
Verify no new commit is created (git log HEAD count unchanged)

criteria.description: Test: git_commit_harness_state handles both tracked modifications and untracked new files in harness-state/
testSteps: Verify a @test adds a new (untracked) file AND modifies an existing file in harness-state/
Verify both changes appear in the commit after calling git_commit_harness_state

criteria.description: Test: generate_pr_body produces markdown containing a sprint results table with correct column headers
testSteps: Verify a @test calls generate_pr_body with fixtures installed (config.json, sprint-plan.json)
Verify the output contains '| Sprint | Name | Status | Criteria | Pass | Fail | Attempts | Cost |'

criteria.description: Test: generate_pr_body includes PASS/FAIL status from eval reports when they exist
testSteps: Verify a @test installs an eval-report-pass.json fixture for a sprint
Verify the output of generate_pr_body includes 'PASS' in the row for that sprint

criteria.description: Test: generate_pr_body shows 'pending' status for sprints without eval reports
testSteps: Verify a @test sets up a sprint-plan with multiple sprints but only provides eval reports for some
Verify sprints without eval reports show 'pending' in their row

criteria.description: Test: generate_pr_body includes configuration summary (model, context strategy, project type)
testSteps: Verify a @test calls generate_pr_body and checks the output includes the model, context strategy, and project type from config.json

criteria.description: Every @test in test-git.bats uses init_test_repo or equivalent git init to ensure test isolation -- no test depends on another test's git state
testSteps: Verify the setup() function or each @test calls init_test_repo (or git init)
Verify no @test references branches or tags that would only exist if another test ran first

criteria.description: Test names follow the pattern 'function_name: behavior description' (e.g., 'git_create_harness_branch: creates branch from main')
testSteps: Inspect all @test names in test-git.bats
Verify each follows 'function_name: descriptive behavior' convention
criteria.threshold: All test names follow the convention

criteria.description: Tests source the git.sh library via source_harness_lib 'git.sh' (not direct source path)
testSteps: Verify test-git.bats calls source_harness_lib 'git.sh' in setup() or at file level
Verify no hardcoded paths to harness/lib/git.sh

outOfScope: git_create_pr and git_create_issue functions (these require the gh CLI and network access; they are out of scope for this pure-git test suite)
Tests for functions in utils.sh (covered in sprints 2 and 3)
Edge case tests beyond the core behaviors (covered in sprint 6)
Modifying the test helper or fixtures (use existing infrastructure from sprint 1)
